description_long: This is a dark brown, open-weave picnic basket constructed from numerous slender wooden rods arranged horizontally to form the sides and base. The basket has a pentagonal, slightly flared shape, with the rods stacked in a consistent, parallel pattern, creating a visually striking geometric effect. A single, thick, twisted handle arches over the top, attached securely at two opposite corners, and is made from the same material as the body, featuring a beaded or knotted texture. The basket is open at the top, with no visible lid or closure mechanism, making its interior easily accessible. The overall construction appears lightweight yet sturdy, suitable for carrying food or personal items in an urban park or outdoor setting. The finish is matte, with a natural wood grain visible throughout, and no additional decorative elements are present.
description: A dark brown, open-weave wooden picnic basket with a twisted handle and a pentagonal, flared shape. The basket is open-topped and features a matte, natural wood finish.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the basket's flared pentagonal shape, open top, and the twisted handle arching overhead.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the parallel rod construction and the attachment points of the handle at the corners.
description_view_2: Front-right view emphasizing the open weave and the beaded texture of the handle.
description_view_3: Rear-right view displaying the basket's geometric structure and the consistent spacing of the wooden rods.
attribute_handle_type: Single, arched, twisted wooden handle, fixed at two corners, beaded texture.
attribute_weave_pattern: Open, parallel rod weave with a geometric, pentagonal layout; medium openness, no decorative variations.
